status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the only active implementation slice.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: All acceptance and verification steps passed with recorded evidence.

features.name: Canonical harness and typed application foundation
features.description: Consolidate product documentation, establish the target Next.js/TypeScript toolchain, and define an incremental migration boundary around the verified prototype.
features.user_visible_behavior: The existing playable baseline remains available while the new application can start and render a stable shell.
verification: Run the harness validator and record a 100/100 structural result after documentation consolidation.
Run the prototype test and production build baseline before replacing any covered module.
Run target lint, typecheck, unit tests, and production build successfully after the typed foundation is introduced.
Confirm there is one canonical document for each product, architecture, frontend, data, reliability, security, and planning concern.
evidence: 2026-07-17: Before refactor, node .agents/skills/harness-creator/scripts/validate-harness.mjs --target . scored 100/100.
2026-07-17: bash init.sh passed; Vite transformed 37 modules. Direct ./init.sh exposed a missing executable bit.
2026-07-17: Source direction docs were consolidated into canonical root-routed docs; duplicate nested AGENTS, v1/v2 design, task, prototype summary, UI plan, and evaluator files were removed.
2026-07-17: Restored init.sh executable metadata; ./init.sh passed 11/11 tests and the production build transformed 37 modules.
2026-07-17: Post-refactor harness validation scored 100/100; 85 Markdown files passed local-link validation; JSON parsing and git diff --check passed.
2026-07-17: Migrated to Next.js 15.5.20, React 19.2.7, TypeScript 5.9.3, and ESLint 9.39.2; the typed shell is served at / and the preserved game at the former compatibility route.
2026-07-17: npm run verify passed ESLint with zero warnings, strict TypeScript checking, 11/11 regression tests, and the production build for / and the former compatibility route.

2026-07-17: Sequential production HTTP checks returned the expected foundation-shell and prototype-loading content. In-app browser control was unavailable; visual evidence is tracked for the UI feature.
2026-07-17: code-reviewer rubric accepted product-foundation with 12/12 and no required fixes.
features.notes: Foundation gate complete. Next.js is the sole build/runtime system; preserve the former compatibility route until equivalent typed replacement coverage exists.

features.name: Executable workout, blueprint, movement, and coaching contracts
features.description: Implement versioned Zod schemas, inferred TypeScript types, fixtures, validation errors, and deterministic fallbacks for every cross-domain payload.
features.user_visible_behavior: Invalid or unavailable AI output never starts a broken mission; users receive a safe fallback or an actionable retry.
verification: Unit-test valid, malformed, boundary, unknown-movement, duplicate-ID, and referential-integrity cases.
Confirm TypeScript types are inferred from schemas rather than duplicated.
Run the standard verification path successfully.
evidence: 2026-07-17: Added strict version-1 Zod schemas and z.infer types for workout, blueprint, movement, metrics, recommendation, and coach payloads.
2026-07-17: Fourteen Vitest cases passed for valid, malformed, boundary, unknown-movement, duplicate-ID, ordering, referential-integrity, fallback, metrics, recommendation, and coach cases.
2026-07-17: npm run verify passed ESLint, strict TypeScript, 11/11 regression tests, 14/14 contract tests, and Next.js production build.
2026-07-17: Dependency audit returned two moderate findings and no high or critical findings after upgrading Vitest to 4.1.10.
2026-07-17: code-reviewer accepted domain-contracts with 12/12 and no required fixes.
features.notes: Contracts are the public cross-domain boundary. Provider and UI features must use resolution helpers; adding movements requires registry, detection, gameplay, and browser evidence.

features.name: Workout planning and adventure briefing experience
features.description: Build the app shell, design system, home, workout input, generation states, and validated adventure briefing.
features.user_visible_behavior: A guest or signed-in user can request a workout and understand the resulting mission, including safe fallback behavior.
verification: Verify the happy path, invalid output, timeout, fallback, retry, and guest paths.
Verify desktop and narrow responsive states plus keyboard access.
Run the standard verification path successfully.
evidence: 2026-07-17: Implemented semantic workout form, loading/error/retry states, deterministic server-only personalization adapter, validated adventure compilation, fallback notices, and responsive briefing UI.
2026-07-17: Five planning-service tests passed for valid generation, scaling, invalid output, rejection, and timeout fallback; full suite passed 11 prototype plus 19 TypeScript tests.
2026-07-17: Live HTTP checks returned a personalized strength plan with two valid segments, rejected malformed input with HTTP 400 and actionable copy, and served the planning page content.
2026-07-17: ./init.sh passed lint, strict typecheck, 30 total tests, and Next.js production build including /api/workout/generate.

2026-07-17: Three happy-dom interaction tests passed for keyboard focus/radio operation, selected-preference submission, generated briefing, announced request failure, retry, and preference preservation.
2026-07-17: Final automated gate passed 11 prototype plus 22 TypeScript tests, lint, strict typecheck, production build, harness validation 100/100, and git diff --check.

2026-07-17: Chrome desktop verification at 1440x1000 confirmed readable form and generated Forge of Strength briefing; actual selections produced 15 minutes, difficulty 2, 150 XP, 18 squats, and 15 jumps with no console errors.
2026-07-17: Chrome narrow verification at 390x844 confirmed stacked form and briefing, 375px document width without horizontal overflow, single-column computed grids, and readable controls/content.

2026-07-17: Chrome keyboard verification confirmed focus order AI Fitness Escape link -> prototype game link -> Goal select; DOM tests cover remaining controls, submission, failure announcement, and retry.
2026-07-17: code-reviewer accepted planning-experience with 12/12 and no required fixes.
features.notes: Provider adapters remain server-side and UI consumes validated results only. Deterministic personalization is production-functional; an external paid AI provider requires an explicit provider/credential decision.

features.name: Welcome, identity entry, and fitness-profile onboarding
features.description: Create the welcome and authentication entry, guest continuation, validated fitness profile, local guest repository, and replaceable account adapter boundary needed before one-time movement setup.
features.user_visible_behavior: A new user can choose account entry or continue as a guest, provide height, weight, activity frequency, goal, fitness level, and optional movement limitations, then resume locally with a validated saved profile; unavailable account services never block guest use.
verification: Verify welcome entry, account-service unavailable/error handling, and guest continuation using keyboard and narrow/desktop layouts without presenting a fake successful sign-in.
Verify required profile validation, unit/range boundaries, optional limitations, save, reload, edit, and privacy explanation.
Verify signed-out or unavailable account services degrade to the local guest repository without losing valid profile input.
Run the standard verification path successfully.
evidence: 2026-07-18: Added a strict versioned Zod fitness-profile schema, browser-local guest repository, stable guest identity, corrupt-data rejection, and replaceable account-service boundary.
2026-07-18: Added welcome, honest account-unavailable recovery, guest profile create/edit/reload, privacy explanation, and /plan handoff with five new repository/UI tests; 38 Vitest tests pass.

2026-07-18: Chrome at 1440x661 verified readable welcome presentation, guest/account actions, account-unavailable status, field validation, saved 181 cm/regular profile, reload continuity, and /plan navigation with zero console errors.
2026-07-18: Chrome at 390x844 verified single-column profile layout with 375px document width and no horizontal overflow; controls and privacy copy remained readable.

2026-07-18: ./init.sh passed ESLint, strict TypeScript, 11 regression tests, 38 Vitest tests, and Next.js production build including /, /plan, /prepare, the former compatibility route, and the planning API.
2026-07-18: Harness validation scored 100/100 and git diff --check passed; code-reviewer accepted the feature 12/12.
features.notes: This slice establishes welcome/profile UI, local guest behavior, contracts, and repository boundaries. Successful remote sign-up/sign-in and sync require configured Supabase credentials and remain owned by progress-persistence.

features.name: Automatic pose platform with migrated squat and jump
features.description: Establish the movement registry, normalized-landmark boundary, touch-free calibration state machine, versioned derived thresholds, and typed event pipeline while migrating reusable squat and jump behavior.
features.user_visible_behavior: Players automatically set up squat and jump once, receive stable tracking feedback, and reuse compatible saved calibration without touching the laptop.
verification: Port and pass the prototype calibration, noise rejection, held-input, and cleanup regression tests.

Verify automatic full-body readiness, countdown, squat/jump sampling, retry, and saved-calibration reuse with deterministic landmark sequences; observe camera permission, tracking, hands-free UI, device recovery, and cleanup in a supported browser.
Measure pose-to-action latency and confirm no per-frame React/global-state updates.
evidence: 2026-07-18: Added a pure automatic coordinator for stable full-body readiness, spoken/visible countdown phases, timed jump/squat sampling, automatic weak-jump retry, and threshold completion without touch input.
2026-07-18: Versioned calibration profiles now persist derived thresholds and pose-model compatibility metadata in local storage; compatible profiles skip repeat camera setup.

2026-07-18: Forty Vitest tests plus eleven regression tests pass, including deterministic no-touch completion, low-visibility rejection, weak-movement retry, calibration math, edge triggering, camera denial/retry, and cleanup.

2026-07-18: Chrome verified camera permission, live tracking, automatic Step into view UI, no lock/capture controls, local-processing copy, and approximately 20.0 ms average inference with no per-frame React state updates.
2026-07-18: User deferred live physical jump/squat completion; TD-005 makes it mandatory during mvp-hardening rather than claiming it passed.
2026-07-18: ./init.sh passed ESLint, strict TypeScript, 51 total tests, and production build; harness validation scored 100/100 and git diff --check passed.
features.notes: Reuse prototype squat/jump math and tests behind new boundaries. This feature creates the platform for the remaining detector-family slices; it does not define the full movement library.

features.name: Lunge and high-knees movement support
features.description: Register, calibrate, classify, and emit typed repetition events for lunge and alternating high knees using the shared standing pose platform.
features.user_visible_behavior: Players can complete lunges and high knees with automatic setup, reliable rep counting, side/alternation feedback, and recoverable tracking guidance.
verification: Verify valid lunge and alternating high-knee repetitions, neutral rearming, held positions, incomplete range, and single-frame noise with recorded landmark fixtures.
Complete automatic lunge/high-knees threshold derivation and rep counting with deterministic landmark sequences; defer the no-touch physical browser journey only through TD-005.
Verify occlusion, insufficient range, and tracking loss produce correction/retry feedback without false credit.
Run the standard verification path successfully.
evidence: 2026-07-18: Expanded the executable movement/challenge registry with lunge, high-knees, lava-stride, and ember-sprint while preserving validated cross-contract mappings.
2026-07-18: Added sample-derived bounded personal thresholds for lunge knee angles and high-knee lift plus clear-sample rejection.
2026-07-18: Added deterministic lunge hold/release/neutral rearm and alternating high-knee emitters with side arbitration, duplicate held-side rejection, confidence validation, and reset behavior.
2026-07-18: Forty-four Vitest tests and eleven regression tests pass, including stable lunge, noisy/incomplete range, alternating high knees, threshold derivation, and contract regressions.
2026-07-18: User-deferred physical no-touch movement evidence remains explicitly mandatory through TD-005 at mvp-hardening.
2026-07-18: ./init.sh passed ESLint, strict TypeScript, 55 total tests, and production build; harness validation scored 100/100 and git diff --check passed.
features.notes: Lunge is a bilateral exercise family for MVP metrics; detector state may track the active leg without creating separate workout IDs.

features.name: Jumping-jack, punch, and side-reach movement support
features.description: Register, calibrate, classify, and emit events for jumping jacks, explicit left/right punches, and explicit left/right side reaches.
features.user_visible_behavior: Players can perform jumping jacks, punch with either arm, and reach to either side with correct directional credit and distance-readable feedback.
verification: Verify complete and partial jumping-jack cycles, left/right punch separation, left/right reach separation, neutral rearming, and held input with recorded landmark fixtures.
Complete automatic setup and live counting for all five movement IDs in a supported browser without touch input.
Verify crossed sides, insufficient arm extension, occlusion, and rapid repeated input do not create false or duplicate credit.
Run the standard verification path successfully.
evidence: 2026-07-18: Expanded the movement/challenge registry with jumping-jack, punch-left/right, side-reach-left/right and five compatible Volcano templates.
2026-07-18: Added sample-derived bounded thresholds for jack foot spread, punch extension, and torso side reach with missing-sample rejection.

2026-07-18: Deterministic emitters verify complete jack cycles, explicit left/right punch credit and neutral rearm, side-reach start/completion, held-input suppression, confidence validation, and reset.
2026-07-18: Forty-eight Vitest tests and eleven regression tests pass; user-deferred live physical evidence remains mandatory through TD-005.
2026-07-18: ./init.sh passed ESLint, strict TypeScript, 59 total tests, and production build; git diff --check passed.
features.notes: Directional IDs are punch-left, punch-right, side-reach-left, and side-reach-right; UI labels may use natural language.

features.name: Push-up and plank floor movement support
features.description: Add automatic side-view floor setup, push-up repetition classification, plank hold/release semantics, and recoverable repositioning guidance.
features.user_visible_behavior: Players can reposition for floor exercises, have side view confirmed automatically, count push-ups, and time planks without returning to the laptop.
verification: Verify push-up depth/rearm and plank start/held/released events using valid, incomplete, misaligned, occluded, and noisy landmark fixtures.
Verify automatic transition from standing setup to side-view floor setup preserves completed calibration and needs no pointer or keyboard input.
Verify live push-up counting, plank timing, wrong-view correction, device loss, retry, and cleanup in a supported browser.
Run the standard verification path successfully.
evidence: 2026-07-18: Expanded the movement/challenge registry with push-up, plank, rising-platform, and shield-hold; the unknown-movement regression now uses unsupported burpee.
2026-07-18: Added distinct side-view readiness based on body-relative camera geometry and sample-derived thresholds for push-up depth, rearm, plank alignment, and view tolerance.

2026-07-18: Deterministic emitters cover stable push-up depth/full extension, duplicate suppression, plank started/held/released timing, misalignment release, wrong-view rejection, confidence validation, and reset.
2026-07-18: Fifty-two Vitest tests and eleven regression tests pass; physical standing-to-floor and real movement evidence remains mandatory through TD-005.
2026-07-18: ./init.sh passed ESLint, strict TypeScript, 63 total tests, and production build; harness validation scored 100/100 and git diff --check passed.
features.notes: Floor movements require a distinct visibility/orientation gate. Do not reuse standing full-body readiness heuristics unchanged.

features.name: Blueprint-driven Phaser mission
features.description: Execute validated adventure segments in Phaser with pose events, objective progress, miss tolerance, scoring, cleanup, and mission completion.
features.user_visible_behavior: The player's workout advances a clear themed mission and ends in a factual session result rather than the prototype endless-runner game over.
verification: Verify every registered movement maps to the correct challenge and progress rule.
Verify recoverable misses, pause/device loss, completion, replay, and cleanup.
Record frame-time evidence and confirm React does not render per Phaser tick.
evidence: 2026-07-18: Added a Phaser 3.90 mission route with blueprint objectives, coarse React HUD snapshots, pause/resume, recovery after three misses, scoring, completion, replay, and teardown.
2026-07-18: Added a validated browser event bridge from typed detector events to the mission controller; malformed and non-matching events cannot advance objectives.
2026-07-18: Controller fixtures map all eleven registered movement IDs, including hold semantics, and cover wrong movement, recovery, pause, replay, and subscription cleanup.
2026-07-18: Chrome rendered exactly one Phaser canvas, pause changed to resume, and 120 scene frames averaged 16.38 ms without per-tick React updates.
2026-07-18: ./init.sh passed ESLint, strict TypeScript, 68 total tests, and the production build.

2026-07-18 TD-005 live audit: automatic full-body framing, jump capture, weak-squat retry, deeper-squat completion, saved thresholds, and 16.6 ms average inference passed. The mission then exposed no camera/detector producer, so physical events could not reach the validated bridge; this feature was reopened.

2026-07-18: Added an objective-aware live mission pose runtime routing every registered movement through vertical, standing-upper, or floor detectors; only target-matching validated events cross into gameplay.
2026-07-18: Mission camera now provides on-device latency, movement cues, full-body/side-view correction, pause on device loss, retry, target reset, and webcam/pose-engine cleanup.

2026-07-18 physical journey: saved setup was reused; 8 controlled squats and 6 jumps produced 14 consecutive credits, 265 XP, 100% completion, 16.5 ms average inference, and factual 14/14 results with 100% accuracy.
2026-07-18: ./init.sh passed ESLint, strict TypeScript, 76 total tests, and production build; harness validation scored 100/100 and git diff --check passed.
features.notes: Reuse prototype state and feedback semantics when compatible; Phaser owns world rendering and its loop.

features.name: Deterministic analysis and grounded AI coaching
features.description: Calculate session metrics and recommendations deterministically, then generate constrained coaching language from those facts.
features.user_visible_behavior: Mission results and coaching agree, remain reproducible, and degrade to useful deterministic copy if the LLM fails.
verification: Unit-test metrics, XP, best/focus exercise, and recommendation boundary rules.
Verify coach output cannot change facts and fallback copy works on timeout or invalid output.
Run the standard verification path successfully.
evidence: 2026-07-18: Added deterministic completion, accuracy, XP, best/focus movement, and bounded recommendation rules over validated workout/adventure facts.
2026-07-18: Added a timeout- and validation-safe coach adapter; provider output may change only the headline while factual and recommendation sentences remain deterministic.
2026-07-18: Added versioned latest-result storage plus responsive /results facts, explicit deterministic-fallback labeling, empty state, and next-mission action.
2026-07-18: Tests cover recommendation boundaries, XP, ties, corrupt storage, rendered facts, invalid provider output, timeout, and attempted provider fact injection.

2026-07-18: ./init.sh passed ESLint, strict TypeScript, 73 total tests, and the production build; a subsequent provider-injection regression brings the current total to 74. Harness validation scored 100/100 and git diff --check passed.
features.notes: The LLM communicates; it does not calculate authoritative values.

features.name: Guest demo golden-journey, accessibility, performance, and privacy hardening

Pass lint, typecheck, unit, integration, Playwright, production build, and documented provider-fallback checks.
Record pose latency, gameplay frame time, reduced-motion, keyboard, responsive, cleanup, privacy, AI fallback, and local reload evidence.
Confirm account sync is labeled unavailable for the demo and the guest journey remains complete without Supabase credentials.

Visual browser acceptance is still pending because the browser plugin connection became unavailable.

The check also exposed and fixed an initialization race so missing/failed tracking now pauses the authoritative mission before offering retry.

features.notes: Hackathon completion is guest-only and must not depend on optional credentials, leaderboard breadth, deep cloud history, or a live failure-path demonstration. Recovery code and automated regressions remain preserved; do not claim remote identity or persistence.

features.name: Engaging Volcano Escape mission presentation
features.description: Turn the functional snapshot-driven mission into a polished hackathon showcase with movement-specific encounters, immediate action feedback, combo/reward energy, cohesive transitions, celebration, and optional sound while preserving authoritative mission state.
features.user_visible_behavior: Every recognized movement visibly affects the Volcano Escape world, successful actions feel rewarding, the next challenge is obvious from several feet away, and mission completion delivers a memorable payoff.
verification: Play a representative mission and verify each detector family produces a distinct readable encounter and immediate success feedback without inventing progress.
Verify objective transitions, combo/XP feedback, pause/recovery, and completion celebration remain synchronized with the authoritative mission snapshot and React HUD.
Verify optional sound, voice coexistence, reduced motion, desktop/narrow layout, cleanup, and a 60 FPS target on the reference browser.
Run the standard verification path successfully and visually review the final game presentation.
evidence: 2026-07-18: Added a typed presentation registry for all eleven movements with distinct boulder, flame gate, broken bridge, lava steps, storm gate, directional wall/vine, low tunnel, and ember storm encounters.

2026-07-18: Added snapshot-derived XP rings, combo surges, miss warnings, encounter transitions, in-world combo/XP, and an exclusive escape-portal completion payoff without moving scoring or progress authority into Phaser.
2026-07-18: Added independent optional procedural sound cues for success, combo, transition, miss, and completion with an accessible opt-in control and cleanup; voice remains independently enabled.
2026-07-18: Generated and integrated project-owned Volcano Escape background and transparent fitness-runner art; provenance and replacement boundaries are documented beside the assets.

2026-07-18: System Chrome drove and asserted all eleven encounter kinds plus completion at 1440x900 and 390x844 under reduced motion, preserving 16:9 layout, no overflow, authoritative HUD synchronization, and under-25-ms 120-frame cadence; active and completion screenshots passed visual review.

2026-07-18: Final ./init.sh passed ESLint, strict TypeScript, 11 regression tests, 78 Vitest tests, eight serialized system-Chrome Playwright checks, and production build. Code review accepted game-polish 12/12.
features.notes: Phaser owns world rendering and moment-to-moment effects. React, pose detection, scoring, and persistence boundaries must not move into the scene. Project-owned generated raster art and procedural encounters/audio are documented and replaceable.

features.name: Goal-aware safe workout generation
features.description: Generate a deterministic five-to-seven movement workout whose selection, order, targets, rest, and concise rationale meaningfully respond to goal, duration, fitness level, activity frequency, and reported movement limitations.
features.user_visible_behavior: Changing the user's intent or relevant profile information produces an observably different, well-structured workout briefing that explains its phases, intensity, exclusions, and safety-aware choices without exposing hidden model reasoning.
verification: Generate general, cardio, strength, and mobility plans and confirm each has a distinct five-to-seven movement structure with warm-up, primary work, peak, and finish phases.
Change duration, fitness level, and activity frequency and confirm targets, rest, and intensity change within safe deterministic bounds.
Enter knee, shoulder, and floor-space limitations and confirm incompatible movements are excluded with a visible factual explanation and a playable replacement.
Force planner invalid output, rejection, and timeout and confirm the fallback remains profile-aware, contract-valid, playable, and honestly labeled.
Run the standard verification path and visually inspect the rationale and briefing at desktop and narrow widths.
evidence: 2026-07-19: Deterministic policy generates distinct general, cardio, strength, and mobility structures; 10/15/20-minute requests produce 5/6/7 validated stages with explicit warm-up, primary, variation, peak, and finish mappings.
2026-07-19: Fitness level and activity frequency scale bounded targets, intensity, and recovery. Curated order plus tests prevent consecutive high-impact stages in every goal plan.

2026-07-19: Recognized knee/impact and shoulder/upper-body considerations exclude incompatible movements and refill with goal-specific standing alternatives; local profile goal, level, activity, and considerations preload into the planner.

2026-07-19: Invalid, rejected, timed-out, goal-mismatched, or policy-incompatible proposals regenerate through the same profile-aware deterministic policy with a validated rationale and honest fallback notice.

2026-07-19: The briefing shows intensity, concise observable reasons, and workout phases instead of raw challenge-template identifiers. System Chrome generated and visually accepted cardio/mobility outcomes at 1440x900 and 390x844 with no overflow.
2026-07-19: npm run verify passed lint, strict typecheck, 6 core tests, 83 Vitest tests, ten serialized Playwright checks, and production build. Code review accepted goal-aware-planning 12/12.

2026-07-19: Corrected lower-body strength templates so 10/15/20-minute plans contain only jumping-jack warm-up, squat/lunge work sets, and a jump finale; no punch movement appears. Exact duration sequences are regression-tested and the full gate passes 84 Vitest tests.
features.notes: Deterministic rules own safety, allowed movements, order, targets, rest, and intensity. AI may phrase bounded presentation copy only after validation and may not provide hidden chain-of-thought or override authoritative values.

features.name: Supabase identity sync, progress, history, and leaderboard
features.description: Connect the identity/profile adapter to Supabase Auth, add RLS-protected profile and calibration sync, idempotent guest conversion, session history, streak, XP, and weekly leaderboard after the guest-first hackathon slice is stable.
features.user_visible_behavior: Users continue to play immediately as guests and can securely retain progress across compatible devices after signing in.
